light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing conditions on the Toccoa River are fair with recent stocking boosting confidence in eggs and attractor patterns. High pressure and low cloud cover create technical fishing requiring careful presentation.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning is best due to moderate sky cover and low wind gusts, supporting trout activity in preferred shaded and structured areas under high pressure.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current flow is 236 cfs with a gage height of 1.23 ft. Water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Skies average 27.64% cover with high pressure at 30.19 inHg and steady trend. Light winds average 1.5 mph and rain chance is low at 29% with minimal expected accumulation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will hold near shade, deeper runs, undercut banks, and structure due to bright and high-pressure conditions. Use smaller natural flies and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell for careful, less aggressive presentations. Caution that bright conditions make trout more cover-oriented and less forgiving.
